nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Peverel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Peverall
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Old French Peurel (Latin Piperellus), a diminutive of Old French pevre ‘pepper’ (Anglo-Norman French peivere, Latin piper), originally perhaps used as a nickname on the Continent but used as a personal name in Anglo-Norman England.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: Maud de Peverel was born about 1090, in High Peak, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Ranulphus de Whittington. She died in 1150, in Whittington, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Peverelは約1090年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアヒグ ピークで生まれた。彼女はRanulphus de Whittingtonとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1150年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Peverel(은)는 대략 1090년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 히그h 피크에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Ranulphus de Whittington(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1150년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
